title: festuca ovina subvar. polita
intro: Festuca ovina subvar. polita, also known as fine fescue, is a type of grass native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of fine fescue include reducing inflammation and soothing skin irritations, as well as aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues and digestive problems. Fine fescue can be used in various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
